contextual.title: Aerosolized Antibiotics and Respiratory Tract Infection in Patients on Mechanical Ventilation
contextual.text:
Summary: The purpose of this study was to determine the effect of aerosolized antibiotics on respiratory infection in mechanically ventilated patients.We hypothesize that aerosolized antibiotics , which achieve high drug concentrations in the airway, would more effectively treat respiratory infection, decrease the need for systemic antibiotics and decrease antibiotic resistance.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 be on mechanical ventilation greater than 3 days 

 greater than or equal to 18 years of age survival greater than 14 days 

 greater than 2 ccs of tracheal secretions/4 hours 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 allergy to drugs, pregnancy

metadata.brief_summary: The purpose of this study was to determine the effect of aerosolized antibiotics on respiratory infection in mechanically ventilated patients.We hypothesize that aerosolized antibiotics , which achieve high drug concentrations in the airway, would more effectively treat respiratory infection, decrease the need for systemic antibiotics and decrease antibiotic resistance.

Ventilator Associated Pneumonia.definition: Serious INFLAMMATION of the LUNG in patients who required the use of PULMONARY VENTILATOR. It is usually caused by bacterial CROSS INFECTION in hospitals.

Respiratory Infection.definition: Invasion of the host RESPIRATORY SYSTEM by microorganisms, usually leading to pathological processes or diseases.
